enforced2026_detail: ABAWD time limit is actively enforced statewide as of 11/1/2025, when the prior statewide pandemic-era waiver (covering up to 117 counties) expired. Effective 12/1/2025, five rural counties (Elliott, Lewis, Magoffin, Martin, Wolfe) regained ABAWD waiver/exempt status through at least FY2026; the remaining 115 of 120 counties are fully enforced. Because some areas remain waived, the honest statewide answer is 'partial' (not blanket 'yes'). The Operation Manual reflects OBBBA/HR1 (2025) changes: tracking codes were revised effective 10/25/2025 — age band expanded (ABAWD tracking for ages 18-65; dependent exemption 'DB Any Child Under 14' replacing the prior under-18 threshold).
formulaDetail: Flat federal default of 80 hours/month (avg ~20 hrs/week). MS 2100.A.3 (OMTL-683, R. 10/25/25) states verbatim: 'Be working 80 hours per month. The employment can be working for money (this includes self-employment), in-kind work, or unpaid (volunteer) work, or any combination of these.' MS 2100.D.2: 'Hours will be systematically converted in order to determine if the member is working 80 hours or more/month.' No SNAP-allotment-divided-by-minimum-wage workfare formula is used for the general volunteer lane.
hours_formula_detail: MS 2100.A.3 allows compliance via paid work, in-kind work, or unpaid (volunteer) work for 80 hrs/month. MS 2100.D.2 confirms systematic conversion to the 80-hour/month threshold. No community-service/workfare formula.
minimum_wage_note: Not applicable to the formula: Kentucky uses the flat federal 80-hour standard, not an allotment/minimum-wage workfare formula. For reference, Kentucky has no state minimum wage above the federal floor, so the applicable minimum wage in 2026 is the federal $7.25/hr (Ky. Rev. Stat. 337.275 ties to the federal rate). Set to 0 per schema convention for federal_80 states.
regional_wages_note: Kentucky has no sub-state regional minimum wages affecting any formula (and no formula applies regardless).
qualifying_org_definition: Not restricted in state policy. MS 2100.A.3 permits 'unpaid (volunteer) work' toward the 80-hour requirement without restricting the type, tax status, or location of the organization. No state-approved volunteer site list, MOU, or registration requirement exists for the general volunteer lane (the FSET-241 Workfare Training Site Agreement under MS 2510 applies only to the separate E&T/Workfare lane).
qualifyingOrgShort: Any organization for which the recipient performs unpaid (volunteer) work; Kentucky imposes no tax-status, location, or registration restriction on the org for the general volunteer lane.
verification_instrument: Written documentation of hours from the organization (e.g., a letter or statement confirming the volunteer hours). No named Kentucky state form exists for community-service/volunteer hours. Per MS 2100.A.3 the caseworker must verify hours of in-kind/unpaid work; a client statement is acceptable when not questionable. Documents are typically uploaded via kynect (the consumer portal; Benefind is the underlying eligibility system).
verification_note: No KY state form number for community-service/volunteer hours was found in the Operation Manual (OMVOLIIA). Named forms exist for other purposes (e.g., FS-704 Statement of Disability or Incapacity), but none govern volunteer-hour reporting. Hours are verified at recertification; client statement accepted if not questionable.
submissionMechanism: kynect online portal (kynect.ky.gov) document upload, or in person/by mail at a county DCBS office. No dedicated volunteer-hours form; org documentation is submitted to the caseworker.
submissionDeadline: No separate monthly filing deadline. Hours of in-kind/unpaid work must be verified at each recertification for the prior months in the certification period (MS 2100.A.3). Separately, households must report within 10 days any change in work hours dropping them below the required 20 hrs/week (MS 2100.A.4).
submission_deadline: Verified at recertification for all prior months in the cert period; 10-day reporting rule applies only to a drop below 20 hrs/week. No standalone monthly volunteer-hours deadline.
remote_note: The Operation Manual does not address whether remote/virtual volunteering counts. MS 2100.A.3 references 'unpaid (volunteer) work' generically with no in-person requirement, but the policy is silent, so this cannot be confirmed.
accepts_outofstate_detail: MS 2100.A.3 refers only to 'unpaid (volunteer) work' with no restriction on the geographic location or registration state of the sponsoring organization, and no approved-site list or MOU requirement exists for the general lane. The policy is formally silent. Under the federal default, an out-of-state 501(c)(3) would operationally be acceptable absent an explicit state restriction, but Kentucky does not affirmatively state this.
required_prestep_detail: No gating prestep for the general volunteer lane. Kentucky's SNAP E&T is all-voluntary, so no Employment Development Plan (EDP), enrollment, county assignment, or provider/site registration is required before unpaid volunteer hours count toward the 80-hour requirement. The FSET-241 Workfare Training Site Agreement (MS 2510) applies only to the separate E&T/Workfare lane, not to self-initiated volunteering.
geographic_waivers: Five rural counties are ABAWD-waived/exempt effective 12/1/2025 through at least FY2026: Elliott, Lewis, Magoffin, Martin, and Wolfe. The prior statewide waiver expired 10/31/2025; all remaining 115 of 120 counties are fully enforced as of 11/1/2025. Manual includes a 'WV Living in a Waived County' tracking code.
geographicWaivers: 5 waived counties (Elliott, Lewis, Magoffin, Martin, Wolfe) effective 12/1/2025; other 115 of 120 counties enforced as of 11/1/2025.
county_variation_note: Aside from the five waiver counties (a binary exempt/enforced distinction), the substantive rules — 80-hour standard, volunteer lane, verification at recertification — are uniform statewide. No material county-by-county variation in how volunteer hours qualify or are reported.
form_fields_note: No named state form; these are the baseline fields a verification letter/statement from the organization should contain to satisfy caseworker review at recertification.
conflictsWithPrior: enforced2026 / abawd_enforced_2026: prior file said 'yes'; corrected to 'partial' because five counties (Elliott, Lewis, Magoffin, Martin, Wolfe) are waived/exempt effective 12/1/2025, so ABAWD is not enforced in 100% of the state. Statewide enforcement otherwise applies to 115 of 120 counties as of 11/1/2025. (Substantively consistent with the prior file's own geographic_waivers note.)
unresolved: No named Kentucky verification form for community-service/volunteer hours could be located; verificationFormName remains 'unknown'.
remoteStance and outOfStateNonprofit are formally silent in policy; treated as silent rather than allowed/disallowed.

FY2025 and FY2026 SNAP E&T State Plan PDFs are FlateDecode-compressed and were not directly parsed; the all-voluntary characterization rests on the Operation Manual and corroborating advocacy/news sources rather than the plan text itself.
notes: VERIFICATION PASS (2026-06-22): Confirmed federal_80 classification against the primary source — Kentucky Operation Manual Volume IIA, MS 2100 (OMTL-683, R. 10/25/25), retrieved and text-extracted directly. MS 2100.A.3 verbatim: 'Be working 80 hours per month. The employment can be working for money (this includes self-employment), in-kind work, or unpaid (volunteer) work, or any combination of these. ... The hours of in-kind/unpaid income must be verified at each recertification for the prior months in between cert periods.' This establishes a general, self-initiated volunteer lane at the flat 80-hour federal standard with NO allotment/minimum-wage workfare formula and NO site-registration prestep. The only site-agreement instrument in the manual (Form FSET-241, Workfare Training Site Agreement, MS 2510) is confined to the separate E&T/Workfare lane. OBBBA/HR1 (2025) implementation is visible in the manual's 10/25/2025 revision: ABAWD tracking codes now cover ages 18-65 and the dependent exemption is 'DB Any Child Under 14' (note that for active cases with any child under 18 prior to 10/25/2025, the old code persists until recertification). Enforcement corrected to 'partial': statewide as of 11/1/2025 except five waiver counties (Elliott, Lewis, Magoffin, Martin, Wolfe) effective 12/1/2025 — confirmed by the CHFS ABAWD county-changes notice and Kentucky Lantern. No named volunteer-hours verification form found (verificationFormName = unknown); client statement accepted if not questionable; documentation uploaded via kynect. remoteStance and outOfStateNonprofit remain silent in policy. Confidence 'high' on classification + 80 hours + general volunteer lane (direct primary-source quotes); the single open item is the absence of a named verification form.
